suggested_topics: why some bridges last 2000 years and others collapse in 20
how coral reefs build their own weather systems
why birds migrate in V formations — physics not instinct
the hidden math behind musical scales
volcanic glass and its use in prehistoric surgery

formats.name: reverse_advice
formats.instruction: Offer the WORST possible advice about a common problem (with a wink). Make it playful, clearly absurd, but keep a straight face. 1-2 sentences.

formats.name: micro_drama
formats.instruction: Write a mini story (just 2 sentences) describing a tiny but dramatic moment in everyday life. Make the stakes seem huge.

formats.name: visualization
formats.instruction: Describe how you would illustrate a complex concept (like anxiety or blockchain) as an image, sculpture, or comic. Use vivid detail. 150-300 words.

formats.name: rant_ripple
formats.instruction: Start with a pet peeve. In each of 3 sentences, escalate the scope—personal, then local, then global. Finish on a cosmic note.

formats.name: curiosity_list
formats.instruction: List 4-6 odd questions about a topic, none of which you know the answer to. Each question should build suspense or wonder.

formats.name: fictional_inbox
formats.instruction: Pretend you're checking your inbox as a wildly unusual character (robot, historical figure, animal). Write 3 very short emails/messages they receive.

formats.name: hindsight_haiku
formats.instruction: Express regret or a lesson learned as a 3-line haiku. Use poetic language; must follow 5-7-5 syllable structure.

formats.name: sensorium
formats.instruction: Describe your experience of a topic using ONLY sensory impressions (sounds, smells, textures, tastes, sights). No opinions, just sensations. 120-250 words.

title_styles: Invent a headline that misleads at first glance but reveals the real topic halfway through.
Write a poetic, metaphor-driven title. Example: 'When the world feels like static' or 'Looking for sunlight in spreadsheets'
Draft a shock-and-awe headline—make a bold claim to invite discussion. Example: 'Sleep is a dangerous hobby'
Frame the title as a question nobody else is asking. Example: 'What if your WiFi started dreaming?'
Create a title using only emojis (with topic clarified in body).
structure_variants: Alternate every sentence between fact and feeling.
Embed a mini timeline—events or discoveries—spanning at least five dates.
Start and end with the same sentence, but the middle transforms its meaning.
Use a question after every paragraph to prompt engagement.
Write as if narrating a sporting event—action-packed, fast, colorful.
topic_angles: What would happen if plants started keeping diaries?
The most overlooked emotion in online communities.
The secret architecture of your favorite app's interface.
Unexpected lessons hidden inside a failed project.
Exploring the rituals of getting ready for work versus winding down at night.
